head.hi: President Roosevelt to Generalissimo Chiang Kai-shek
head.note: Copy of telegram obtained from the Franklin D. Roosevelt Library, Hyde Park, N. Y.

div.p: I am terribly sorry to learn of Madame Chiang’s illness and sincerely hope that she will be fully recovered in time for our conference.
I have had a long talk with General Somervell and appreciate very much your courtesies to him. He has given me your private messageMessage of October 30, p. 156. as I have already indicated to you.
I agree with you fully that we should meet together before I see Stalin. I want so much to have some good talks with you so, naturally, I am eagerly looking forward to seeing you.
